name: Enlighten (Advanced Terminal Progress Bars)

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-line terminal progress bar library. import enlighten. manager = enlighten.get_manager(). pbar = manager.counter(total=100, desc='Processing', unit='items'). for i in range(100): do_work(); pbar.update(). Multiple concurrent bars: bar1 = manager.counter(); bar2 = manager.counter(). Status bar: status = manager.status_bar('Working...'). Print without corruption: manager.print('message'). Custom format: counter(bar_format='{desc}{desc_pad}{count}/{total} |{bar}|'). Autorefresh. Thread-safe. Works well in loops with nested progress bars. Doesn't require explicit close with context manager. Less popular than tqdm but better for multiple concurrent bars.

gotchas: MPL-2.0 license — copyleft for modified versions of the file
Less popular than tqdm — smaller community
manager.stop() needed at end to restore terminal
Rich's Progress is a more modern alternative with the same capabilities
best_when: Long-running tasks needing non-corrupting print output alongside progress
When tqdm's single-bar limitation causes issues
avoid_when: When Rich's Progress is already in the dependency set
summary: Enlighten provides multi-line terminal progress bars. manager = enlighten.get_manager(); counter(total=100, desc='...'). Multiple concurrent bars. Status bar. Thread-safe print without corruption. MPL-2.0. Less popular than tqdm. Best for nested or concurrent progress bars. Rich's Progress is a more modern alternative. Use tqdm for simple single-bar needs.
